fingerprints: Loans are only permitted in the case of financial hardship
The interest rate on all loans will be 1% over the Prime Rate
Scheduled payments must be made by payroll deduction
This Loan Administration Policy has been developed to comply with the requirements of Internal Revenue Code section 72(p)

tables.name: Loan Fees and Charges
tables.header_pattern: (Section 2\.01 A loan origination fee in the amount of \$50\.00 shall be deducted from the loan amount approved\.)

tables.name: Loan Types and Terms
tables.section_context: Article VII. Types of loans available
tables.header_pattern: (Section 7\.01 A General Purpose Loan has a term of twelve to sixty \(12-60\) months\.)

Loan Purpose Restrictions: Loans are only permitted in the case of financial hardship. Applicants must submit proof of the hardship to the Plan Administrator. For this purpose, financial hardship is as defined under
Loan Offset Options After Termination: Option A. In the event that a participant defers distribution of their account balance, a loan offset will occur no later than the end of the month following the 60th
Option B. The participant may continue to repay the loan by making loan repayments to the Employer. The Employer will deposit the loan repayments in the Trust through the Employer’
Partial Loan Prepayments: Option A - Advance Payments Through Payroll Withholding - A participant may choose to make two or more payments in advance through payroll withholding. These payments will be applied as follows
According to the current amortization schedule. Payments may not be made more than six months in advance
First to the current payment due, and then to the outstanding principal of the loan. The maturity date of the loan will be accelerated
Option B - Principal Reduction - A participant may make a payment of principal reduction. The amount will be applied first to any payments due and then to the outstanding principal of
